segments.text: Then Elihu continued:
Hear my words, O wise men; give ear to me, O men of learning.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For the ear tests wordsas the mouth tastes food.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Let us choose for ourselves what is right; let us learn together what is good.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For Job has declared, ‘I am righteous, yet God has deprived me of justice.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
Would I lie about my case? My wound is incurable,
though I am without transgression.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: What man is like Job, who drinks up derision like water?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He keeps company with evildoers and walks with wicked men.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For he has said, ‘It profits a man nothingthat he should delight in God.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Therefore listen to me, O men of understanding. Far be it from God to do wrong, and from the Almighty to act unjustly.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For according to a man’s deeds He repays him; according to a man’s ways He brings consequences.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Indeed, it is true that God does not act wickedly, and the Almighty does not pervert justice.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Who gave Him charge over the earth? Who appointed Him over the whole world?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If He were to set His heart to it and withdraw His Spirit and breath,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: all flesh would perish together and mankind would return to the dust.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If you have understanding, hear this; listen to my words.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Could one who hates justice govern? Will you condemn the just and mighty One,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: who says to kings, ‘You are worthless!’and to nobles, ‘You are wicked,’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
who is not partial to princes and does not favor rich over poor?
For they are all the work of His hands.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They die in an instant, in the middle of the night. The people convulse and pass away; the mighty are removed without human hand.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For His eyes are on the ways of a man, and He sees his every step.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: There is no darkness or deep shadowwhere the workers of iniquity can hide.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: For God need not examine a man furtheror have him approach for judgment.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: He shatters the mighty without inquiry and sets up others in their place.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Therefore, He recognizes their deeds; He overthrows them in the night and they are crushed.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: He strikes them for their wickednessin full view,
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: because they turned aside from Him and had no regard for any of His ways.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: They caused the cry of the poor to come before Him, and He heard the outcry of the afflicted.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: But when He remains silent, who can condemn Him? When He hides His face, who can see Him? Yet He watches over both man and nation,
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text:
that godless men should not rule
or lay snares for the people.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Suppose someone says to God,‘I have endured my punishment; I will offend no more.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Teach me what I cannot see; if I have done wrong, I will not do it again.’
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Should God repay you on your own termswhen you have disavowed His? You must choose, not I; so tell me what you know.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Men of understanding will declare to me, and the wise men who hear me will say:
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: ‘Job speaks without knowledge; his words lack insight.’
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: If only Job were tried to the utmostfor answering like a wicked man.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text:
For he adds rebellion to his sin; he claps his hands among us
and multiplies his words against God.
segments.speaker: Elihu
